nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as sole creator of lesson content
nodes.grounding_span: I take the ideas and lesson plans generated by the AI as the first step of the building block and then I build upon it

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I could feed the AI model my ideas and evidence of learning for the student, and it would help me word the report professionally

nodes.label: Teacher-led student assessment and feedback
nodes.grounding_span: I strongly feel that should still come from a teacher

nodes.label: AI as tool for overcoming creative block
nodes.grounding_span: the AI getting the ball rolling with the idea can help me to keep going

nodes.label: Teacher authenticity and professional identity
nodes.grounding_span: keeping a human element and personality to my lessons and reports

nodes.label: Efficiency and reduced burnout
nodes.grounding_span: keeping my skills sharp, while also not burning out

nodes.label: Student engagement and learning
nodes.grounding_span: what would be the most engaging to them... what it would practically take to set up

nodes.label: Positive toward AI for report writing

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I would like to explore resource creation with AI more

nodes.label: Collaborative curation — selects and builds upon AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I pick one that I like, and I ask it to expand it into a full lesson plan
